summary: REGRESSION CONFIRMATION — RunEvalModal testing_agent_bench submissions now ALWAYS stamp a top-level `agent_name` on the POST to /api/eval/testing-agent-evals. Verified end-to-end in the PREVIEW environment (https://ui-preview-debug.internal.preview.emergentagent.com) via Playwright request-interception. All three scenarios pass: (1) no override — derives from dataset's attributes.agent_name; (2) explicit override — top-level + items[].agent_name both equal the override; (3) new group row on /evals renders the agent_name as a blue badge.
action_items: Re-deploy the cloud build so https://ui-preview-debug.internal.emergent.host (production) picks up RunEvalModal.js line 768-769 (batchBody.agent_name = derivedAgentName || items[0].agent_name). The fix is confirmed in preview; user's report is against the stale production bundle (last cloud build failed).
Optional: leave the two TEST_ groups in MongoDB or delete them — they were real submissions against the harness.
critical_code_review_comments: RunEvalModal.js:708-712 derivedAgentName chain (override → initialAgentName prop → selectedProblems[0].attributes.agent_name) correctly resolves at submit time so the field is never silently dropped on N>1 runs.

RunEvalModal.js:768-769 fork-eval branch now stamps batchBody.agent_name = derivedAgentName || items[0].agent_name — covers BOTH the override and the dataset-attribute path. Captured payloads show top-level agent_name on every request.

Each items[i].agent_name is independently set at line 728 (trimmedAgentOverride || attrs.agent_name) — when override is provided it propagates to every item, when absent each item carries its own dataset agent.
seed_data_creation: Two real eval groups submitted against the harness: (a) 'TEST_verify_agent_name_top' — 1 job, dataset testing_agent_bench/1313151e-dc80-4a7a-880f-187dba1afe6b, agent_name=full_stack_app_builder_cloud_v8_sonnet_4_5 (dataset-derived); (b) 'TEST_verify_agent_name_override' — 1 job, same dataset, agent_name=TEST_override_agent_v3_codex (explicit override). Both visible on /evals top-of-list. Per instructions, NOT cancelled.
context_for_next_testing_agent: RunEvalModal testids for testing_agent_bench flow: new-eval-btn → run-eval-modal → dataset-type-filter (option 'Testing Agent Bench') → preview-<name> rows (click row, not preview button) → eval-next-step → eval-group-name + eval-testing-agent-name-override → eval-next-step → submit-eval-button. POST endpoint to intercept: /api/eval/testing-agent-evals. Payload schema: {group_name, items: [{prod_job_id, agent_name, hitl_input, golden_output, model_name?}], agent_name (top-level), comment?, user_id?, judge_prompt?, judge_model?}. The two TEST_ groups remain in the DB; they make a useful smoke-test corpus.

captured_payloads.test3_group_badge: Blue badge with class 'bg-blue-500/5 border-blue-... font-mono' rendered on both new group rows, text matches each group's agent_name. Verified via DOM query for the override string.

rca of the issue: User's bug report came from the PRODUCTION host ui-preview-debug.internal.emergent.host where the last cloud build failed → the deployed bundle still has the OLD code path that only set agent_name from override (and dropped it when override was empty). The preview env (ui-preview-debug.internal.preview.emergentagent.com) already has the iter-36 fix at RunEvalModal.js:768-769 (`batchAgent = derivedAgentName || items[0].agent_name`; `if (batchAgent) batchBody.agent_name = batchAgent`). This iteration formally confirms the preview's outgoing POST contains a non-empty top-level agent_name in both override-present and override-absent scenarios. No code change needed in preview; main agent should trigger a fresh cloud deploy so prod picks up the fix.
